commit.message:
Add first-run setup wizard, auto-generate SECRET_KEY (#5)

* Reapply "Add first-run setup wizard, auto-generate SECRET_KEY"

This reverts commit f47f641a2ef1cdd25aac273e675eae3f03b284e3.

* Address PR review comments: fix audit log ordering, add username uniqueness check, use Depends(get_current_user), fix entrypoint SECRET_KEY handling, fix docs

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/pacnpal/callis/sessions/a6a196c4-a1e3-4f43-9ad5-737735c486e3

Co-authored-by: pacnpal <183241239+pacnpal@users.noreply.github.com>

* Enforce file permissions on every boot, not just creation

- chmod 700 /data on startup (mounted volume may have loose defaults)
- chmod 600 /data/.secret_key on every boot (not just when created)
- chmod 600 /data/callis.db if it exists (SQLite DB contains hashed
  passwords and encrypted TOTP secrets)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update all docs for setup wizard, auto SECRET_KEY, and boot hardening

- README: Add setup wizard to features, simplify Quick Start (no .env
  needed), update Security Notes with file permission enforcement
- ARCHITECTURE: Add setup_guard.py middleware, setup.py router,
  setup templates to layouts and file structure; add first-run
  request flow diagram
- DEPLOYMENT: Remove ADMIN_PASSWORD/ADMIN_USERNAME/SECRET_KEY as
  required; update Mode A for zero-config start; remove nonexistent
  Caddy sidecar mode; fix DATABASE_URL format; add missing env vars
- DEVELOPMENT: Remove .env copy step, mention setup wizard
- REQUIREMENTS: Update FR-DEPLOY-01 (no .env required), FR-USER-07
  (setup wizard, not env vars)
- SECURITY: Add section 1.2 for first-run setup security contracts
  and file permission hardening on every boot

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix admin.id flush, atomic secret key file creation, and umask subshell scoping

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/pacnpal/callis/sessions/d86af837-8a71-4b2b-8f13-3836da9eebb6

Co-authored-by: pacnpal <183241239+pacnpal@users.noreply.github.com>

* Narrow TOTP exemption, guard totp_secret None, fail-fast empty key file, optional .env, fix perms in read path, clarify SECURITY.md

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/pacnpal/callis/sessions/cd48a030-f0a3-43ae-9e7d-be3bd08f815e

Co-authored-by: pacnpal <183241239+pacnpal@users.noreply.github.com>

* Protect SECRET_KEY rotation, add one-time setup CSRF token, race-condition fix, fix SECURITY.md enforcement wording

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/pacnpal/callis/sessions/f53aaaf8-02d8-4164-af9a-70ecfce7ae80

Co-authored-by: pacnpal <183241239+pacnpal@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove setup token; advise users to complete setup immediately on first run

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/pacnpal/callis/sessions/cd4f9248-a0c1-4592-9000-c717547870f0

Co-authored-by: pacnpal <183241239+pacnpal@users.noreply.github.com>

* Lock down setup routes once admin enrolled; add startup warning; update README

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/pacnpal/callis/sessions/e9095c37-b520-4780-b529-76ceba1eae55

Co-authored-by: pacnpal <183241239+pacnpal@users.noreply.github.com>

* Apply review feedback: fix _is_fully_setup, remove redundant flag, fix README/Compose versioning, warn on chmod failure

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/pacnpal/callis/sessions/7f9eacba-cdf2-4b95-a1bf-2d4ae3e82b61

Co-authored-by: pacnpal <183241239+pacnpal@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix guarded chmod on SECRET_KEY_FILE and callis.db; clarify .env.example is optional

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix 3 Copilot review comments: key rotation guard, PermissionError handling, multi-worker safety docs

- Add mismatch check in _resolve_secret_key() when SECRET_KEY env var is set
  and a persisted /data/.secret_key already exists with a different value.
  Raises ValueError unless CALLIS_ROTATE_SECRET_KEY=true, mirroring
  entrypoint.sh behaviour.
- Extract _fix_key_file_permissions() helper that catches PermissionError/OSError
  on chmod, and wrap os.makedirs + os.open in try/except with clear warnings for
  non-Docker environments where /data may not be writable.
- Add detailed multi-worker safety comment on _setup_lock explaining why the
  asyncio.Lock + in-transaction re-check + IntegrityError catch is sufficient
  for both SQLite (single-writer) and PostgreSQL (layers 2+3) deployments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix 2 Copilot review comments: unconditional secret key resolution, setup race doc

- api/core.py: get_settings() now always calls _resolve_secret_key()
  regardless of whether SECRET_KEY was set via env, ensuring the
  file-vs-env mismatch check and rotation guard run in all cases
- api/routers/setup.py: expanded inline comment at the COUNT(*) re-check
  to document the intentional design decision (SQLite single-writer is
  sufficient; PostgreSQL unique constraints + IntegrityError catch
  provide defense-in-depth)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix 3 Copilot review comments: safe cat error handling, FileNotFoundError split

- entrypoint.sh: wrap both `cat "$SECRET_KEY_FILE"` calls in explicit
  error handlers so a read failure under `set -e` prints a FATAL message
  instead of silently exiting; also strip CR/LF from persisted key before export
- api/core.py: split FileNotFoundError out of the broad OSError catch in
  _fix_key_file_permissions so first-run (no file yet) is silent; only warn
  on real permission/FS errors; add path parameter for clarity

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix 2 Copilot review comments: exact path matching in setup guard, SECRET_KEY doc

- setup_guard.py: Split exempt check into exact paths (/setup) and prefix
  matches (/static/, /health) so /setup/totp is no longer exempt from the
  guard — unauthenticated requests to /setup/totp when no users exist now
  redirect to /setup instead of hitting the auth dependency
- SECURITY.md: Clarify SECRET_KEY generation mentions both Docker path
  (openssl rand -hex 32 via entrypoint.sh) and API fallback
  (secrets.token_hex(32)), both persisted to /data/.secret_key

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(ux): kanban auto-scroll, touch drag, leads mobile responsive

- Kanban auto-scroll: SPEED 20→35, EDGE dinâmico 14% da largura,
  curva clamped^0.65 para acelerar mais rápido ao entrar na zona
- Kanban drag: desabilita scrollBehavior+scrollSnapType no drag start,
  restaura no drag end (elimina conflito snap vs rAF loop)
- TouchSensor tolerance 5→10 em Kanban e KanbanPosVenda
- touch-action:none nos cards draggáveis (LeadCard + CardPosVenda)
- Leads: card-list responsivo em mobile (<md) com nome, etapa,
  temperatura e próxima ação; tabela 6 colunas mantida em ≥md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: reload core.vector_store if _MissingPackage pollutes it on Python 3.10

On Python 3.10, unittest.mock.patch propagates ImportError from
_MissingPackage.__getattr__ instead of catching it as AttributeError
(which getattr() guards against). When test_optional_dependency_guards
runs before test_search_does_not_load_all_rows_beyond_limit, the
core.vector_store module is left with np=_MissingPackage, causing
patch('core.vector_store.np.frombuffer', ...) to fail.

Fix: detect the pollution at the top of the test and reload the
module so it re-imports real numpy from sys.modules.

Also fix unterminated string literals in path/to/linter_script.py.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Phase 4 Complete: Add Chronicle integration documentation and update main README

- Add PHASE4_CHRONICLE.md with comprehensive documentation
  - Explains /chronicle client and code analysis integration
  - Shows before/after examples of output improvements
  - Documents pattern detection algorithms
  - Lists all 15 tests and explains graceful degradation

- Update main README.md to highlight Phase 4 features
  - Add code analysis and /chronicle to feature list
  - Explain Phase 4 in architecture section
  - Link to Phase 4 documentation

Impact:
- Reports now include 'Key Changes & What Matters Most To You' section
- Time savings grounded in actual code patterns found in user's repo
- Affected files shown with line numbers
- Actionable next steps specific to user's codebase

Status: Phase 4 COMPLETE
- 5 new modules created (340 KB)
- 15 comprehensive tests (all passing)
- Full pipeline integration
- Documentation complete

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 4: Add /chronicle integration and code analysis for repository-grounded personalization

Features implemented:
- src/chronicle/client.py: ChronicleClient for fetching user development patterns
  - Caches with 30-min TTL to avoid repeated API calls
  - Graceful fallback to git history if /chronicle unavailable
  - Extracts recent files, workflow patterns, error patterns, focus areas

- src/insights/code_analyzer.py: CodeAnalyzer for detecting code patterns
  - Detects async/await issues, error handling, testing, code organization, performance
  - Scans top 30 files by recency to keep performance reasonable
  - Returns concrete examples with file paths and line numbers

- src/insights/pattern_matcher.py: PatternMatcher for linking features to user code
  - Maps changelog features to specific code patterns found in repo
  - Estimates real time savings based on pattern frequency
  - Calculates confidence scores for pattern matches

- Enhanced src/generation/use_cases.py with personalization
  - New _generate_personalized_impact() method for 'Why This Matters To YOU' section
  - Integrates code analysis and chronicle context
  - Generates next steps specific to user's codebase

commit.message:
feat: Harden security policy and introduce code_search tool enhancements (#442)

* fix(security): harden SecurityPolicy command and path validation

- Implement iterative URL decoding (3 levels) in is_path_allowed.
- Block residual '%' and '\' in is_path_allowed.
- Enforce exact command matching in is_segment_valid to block ./cmd bypasses.
- Validate command arguments against path traversal and forbidden paths.
- Expand blocked operators to include glob symbols and backslashes.
- Add comprehensive tests for the new security boundaries.

SONAR:SEC-001

Co-authored-by: yacosta738 <33158051+yacosta738@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(runtime): add native code_search tool

Add a workspace-scoped code_search tool with structured results, regex support, and file safety rules so agents can search code without shell grep hacks.

* chore(runtime): format rust files for pre-push checks

* fix(runtime): resolve pre-push rust warnings

* fix(runtime): tighten code_search validation and docs

Align code_search security ordering docs, reject unknown tool args, cap structured result payloads, and remove temp-file collisions in CLI tests.

* chore(runtime): format code_search review fixes

* fix(runtime): remove dead assignment in code_search

* fix(runtime): simplify code_search output truncation flow

* fix(spec): update security chain to include action recording before path resolution

* fix(spec): clarify path traversal rejection criteria in security policy

* Initial plan

* feat: add code_search benchmarks and freshness strategy doc

- benches/agent_benchmarks.rs: replace empty stub with five criterion
  benchmarks covering literal search, regex search, no-match walk,
  scoped-path search, and include-glob filtering; each benchmark asserts
  correct results to ensure it measures meaningful behavior
- docs/design/code-search-tool.md: add Section 7 (Freshness Strategy)
  documenting the v1 read-from-disk guarantee, agent workflow
  implications, and what a future v2 index would need to define

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/dallay/corvus/sessions/37974b4b-4ad6-45b4-ad94-69a6c69aa73d

Co-authored-by: yacosta738 <33158051+yacosta738@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(runtime): add workspace trigram index persistence

Add shared workspace corpus discovery and a SQLite-backed trigram index with deterministic exclusions, workspace-relative file identities, and refresh-or-rebuild lifecycle support for local code search.

* fix(runtime): satisfy clippy unreadable literal check

* feat(transcription): implement real whisper transcriber for Unix and enhance audio pipeline tests

* fix(runtime): restore security policy test expectations

* fix: apply CodeRabbit auto-fixes

Fixed 13 file(s) based on 16 unresolved review comments.

Co-authored-by: CodeRabbit <noreply@coderabbit.ai>

* fix(runtime): address search and policy review findings

Align search docs with runtime behavior, harden path argument normalization, tighten index artifact handling, and route audio admission tests through the shared helper path.

* chore(runtime): format review follow-up changes

* fix(runtime): remove unused search index import

* fix(runtime): allow public search reexports in bin build

* fix(runtime): align policy and discovery limit behavior

* fix(runtime): preserve shell vars and search cap warnings

* fix(runtime): harden search index and path validation

Prefer transcriber durations, make index discovery deterministic, reject symlinked index paths, tighten env-path validation, and surface fatal code_search discovery failures.

* chore(runtime): format whisper cli contract test

* fix(runtime): align search and env var test behavior

commit.message:
fix: harden idle standby guard against false timeout (#120)

Two changes to prevent false standby while audio is playing:

1. _sink_monitor_active() no longer requires bluetooth_sink_name — the
   fallback daemon-flag timer is suppressed as soon as the PA monitor
   loop is running, not only after the BT sink is discovered. This
   closes a race window where server_connected=True could start the
   fallback timer before sink registration.

2. _idle_timeout() now checks daemon playing/audio_streaming flags as a
   secondary safety net after the PA sink state check. If the daemon
   reports active playback, standby is suppressed even when the sink
   monitor missed a state transition.

Enhanced logging in _idle_timeout prints full diagnostic state (sink
monitor status, sink name, PA state, daemon flags) when entering standby.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial commit: Copilot Changelog Skill - Complete implementation

- Phase 1: Parallel data retrieval (fleet orchestrator + 3 tasks)
- Phase 2: Ranking & relevance scoring (3-dimensional algorithm)
- Phase 3: Use case generation (actionable workflow improvements)
- Phase 4: Markdown report output (focused, top-5 recommendations)
- Phase 5: Skill integration & testing (PowerShell wrapper + tests)

Features:
- Analyzes GitHub Copilot changelog updates
- Scores by workflow impact for YOUR repository
- Auto-detects tech stack (Node, Python, Go, Rust, etc.)
- Generates actionable reports (5-15 seconds)
- Cross-platform (Windows, macOS, Linux)
- Zero external API keys required

Deliverables:
- 16 Python modules (~1,600 lines)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Doezer/Questarr/sessions/765629b8-fbb0-429f-b873-4092d4c4639c

* Fix failing tests

* Upgrade to vite 8 to fix tests

* chore: add .npmrc to allow legacy peer deps for @codecov/vite-plugin

@codecov/vite-plugin@1.9.1 declares peer dep vite@"4.x || 5.x || 6.x"
but the project now uses vite@8. The plugin works at runtime; this flag
unblocks npm ci until an official Vite 8 compatible release is published.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(docker): copy .npmrc so npm ci resolves peer deps correctly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove @codecov/vite-plugin

Plugin had no Vite 8 peer dep support, requiring legacy-peer-deps workaround.
Bundle analysis was optional; coverage upload still works via codecov-action in CI.
Reverts .npmrc addition and Dockerfile changes introduced for the workaround.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* bump package lock

commit.message:
Production-ready fintech backend with comprehensive documentation

Major Changes:
- Complete dashboard overhaul with 7 tabs (Overview, Payments, Merchants, Analytics, Fraud Detection, Ledger, Admin)
- Added ML fraud detection visualization with real-time scoring
- Added hash-chained ledger explorer with integrity verification
- Implemented role-based UI (admin tab visible only to admins)
- Added ledger API endpoints (list blocks, verify chain, get stats)
- Enhanced auth API with /me endpoint for user profile

Documentation:
- Comprehensive README with architecture diagrams and flowcharts
- Added LEDGER_EXPLAINED.md for SHA-256 hash-chain deep dive
- Documented all core services (fraud.py, ledger.py, payment.py)
- Documented API routes (auth.py, payments.py, ledger.py)
- Documented models and security utilities
- Added inline comments and docstrings throughout

Code Cleanup:
- Removed 22 redundant files (15 docs, 5 debug scripts, 2 backups)
- Deleted duplicate documentation
- Removed test/debug scripts from root
- Cleaned up static folder (removed backups)
- Streamlined from 40+ root files to 16 clean files

Features Highlighted:
- ML-powered fraud detection (Isolation Forest)
- SHA-256 hash-chained audit ledger
- 4-tier RBAC (Viewer → User → Analyst → Admin)
- 50+ REST API endpoints
- JWT authentication + API key auth
- Rate limiting, idempotency, webhooks
- MFA, KYC, multi-currency support

Test Status: 139/141 passing (2 pre-existing failures)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Normalize session user model across login flows

- persist canonical user shape for manual and Google login

- unify user-id resolution for profile/education APIs

- align token storage with remember-me behavior

- simplify encrypted storage and remove stale fallback artifacts

commit.message:
Update supabaseClient.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: sync unmute to MA after BT reconnect (#132)

When the daemon subprocess unmutes the PulseAudio sink after a Bluetooth
reconnect, MA still holds the previous muted=True state, causing UI
desync (Bridge shows unmuted, MA shows muted, audio plays normally).

Add _sync_unmute_to_ma() that detects sink_muted→False transitions in
_read_subprocess_output and forwards the unmute to MA via the existing
send_player_cmd WebSocket API. The sync only fires when MUTE_VIA_MA is
enabled, MA is connected, and the muted states are actually desynced.

Fixes #132

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
